title: Prognostic outcomes of pancreaticoduodenectomy with lymph node dissection in pancreatic and periampullary adenocarcinoma; a retrospective cohort study.
abstract: Pancreaticoduodenectomy (PD) is a key surgical treatment for pancreatic and periampullary malignancies, but morbidity remains a challenge. Lymph node dissection improves outcomes by removing cancerous tissue and preventing metastasis. This study aims to assess survival rates, recurrence, and other postoperative outcomes.
trial_name: Based on extrapolation from pancreatic cancer data
